description: Make LAN server discovery reliable under DHCP by introducing a stable server_id, auto-selecting a unique discovered server, restoring the previously selected server across app restarts, and keeping manual host URLs as a non-blocking fallback across macOS, Windows, and Android.

userStories.title: Server: persist a stable server_id and expose it on health
userStories.description: As a client developer, I need each LAN server to expose a durable server_id so clients can recognize the same server after its DHCP address changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Generate and persist a stable server_id on the server host so it survives process restarts unless server storage or config is explicitly reset
GET /health returns the same server_id value used by discovery clients for identity verification
Server startup logs include server_id for diagnostics
go test ./... passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Server: advertise server_id in mDNS metadata
userStories.description: As a discovery client, I need the Go server's mDNS advertisement to include server_id so I can match discovered endpoints to a stable server identity before or immediately after connecting.
acceptanceCriteria: mDNS TXT records include server_id alongside existing version and auth metadata
mDNS advertisement logs show enough detail to confirm server_id is being broadcast
Existing clients that only read version and auth continue to behave safely until upgraded
go test ./... passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Shared contract: define discovery selection sources and restore rules
userStories.description: As a developer, I need one shared selection contract for LAN discovery so all three clients make the same auto-select, restore, and fallback decisions.
acceptanceCriteria: Document and encode one shared contract covering unique-server auto-select, persisted server_id restore on app restart, multi-server no-auto-pick, and manual fallback preservation
Selection logic distinguishes at least these active connection sources: auto_discovered, restored_selection, and manual_fallback
No client silently replaces an explicit manual choice with a different discovered server when multiple servers are present
Repo docs and comments reference scripts/ralph source-of-truth files for this iteration
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: macOS: persist selected server by server_id
userStories.description: As a macOS user, I want the app to remember which LAN server I selected by identity instead of by a stale IP address.
acceptanceCriteria: macOS persists selected server_id plus enough last-known metadata to render the current selection cleanly
Stored selection no longer depends only on host and port equality
The app persists whether the active endpoint came from auto discovery, restored selection, or manual fallback
swift build succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: macOS: start LAN discovery on launch and restore selection
userStories.description: As a macOS user, I want LAN discovery to run on startup so the app can reconnect after DHCP changes without waiting for me to open Config.
acceptanceCriteria: LAN discovery starts automatically on app launch or LAN mode entry, not only while Config is open
If exactly one server is discovered and there is no prior selection, macOS auto-selects it and updates hostURL
If a persisted server_id is rediscovered at a new host or port after restart, macOS restores that selection and updates hostURL automatically
If multiple servers are discovered and there is no prior selection, macOS does not auto-pick one
swift build succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: macOS: show discovery state and manual fallback in Config UI
userStories.description: As a macOS user, I want the Config view to explain whether the current LAN endpoint was auto-discovered, restored, or entered manually.
acceptanceCriteria: Config UI lists discovered servers with enough detail to distinguish them, including selection state and auth requirement
Config UI shows whether the active connection source is auto_discovered, restored_selection, or manual_fallback
When multiple servers are present with no prior selection, the UI shows a passive instruction to open Config and choose one instead of presenting a popup
User can explicitly switch between a discovered server selection and manual URL entry
swift build succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Windows: persist selected server by server_id
userStories.description: As a Windows user, I want the app to remember my LAN server by stable identity so an IP change does not break the saved selection.
acceptanceCriteria: Windows persists selected server_id plus last-known display metadata
Stored selection no longer depends only on host and port equality
The app persists whether the active endpoint came from auto discovery, restored selection, or manual fallback
dotnet build succeeds on a Windows host
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Windows: start LAN discovery on launch and restore selection
userStories.description: As a Windows user, I want startup discovery to recover from DHCP host changes without making me re-enter the host URL after restart.
acceptanceCriteria: LAN discovery starts automatically on app launch or LAN mode entry
If exactly one server is discovered and there is no prior selection, Windows auto-selects it and updates HostUrl
If a persisted server_id is rediscovered at a new host or port after restart, Windows restores that selection and updates HostUrl automatically
If multiple servers are discovered and there is no prior selection, Windows does not auto-pick one or show a blocking chooser
dotnet build succeeds on a Windows host
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Windows: show discovery state and manual fallback in UI
userStories.description: As a Windows user, I want the UI to make it obvious whether I am connected through automatic discovery, a restored selection, or a manual fallback URL.
acceptanceCriteria: Main or config UI lists discovered servers with enough information to distinguish them, including selection state and auth requirement
UI shows whether the active connection source is auto_discovered, restored_selection, or manual_fallback
When multiple servers are present with no prior selection, the UI shows passive guidance instead of a popup
User can explicitly switch between a discovered server selection and manual HostUrl entry
dotnet build succeeds on a Windows host
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Android: persist selected server by server_id
userStories.description: As an Android user, I want the app to remember my chosen LAN server by identity so DHCP changes do not invalidate the saved target.
acceptanceCriteria: Android persists selected server_id plus enough last-known metadata to render the current selection
Stored selection no longer depends only on host or port string equality
The app persists whether the active endpoint came from auto discovery, restored selection, or manual fallback
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Android: start LAN discovery automatically and restore selection
userStories.description: As an Android user, I want LAN discovery to start automatically when the app enters LAN mode so restarts recover from DHCP changes without manual rescanning.
acceptanceCriteria: Discovery starts automatically when the relevant app or service lifecycle enters LAN mode, without requiring the user to tap a scan button
If exactly one server is discovered and there is no prior selection, Android auto-selects it and updates hostUrl
If a persisted server_id is rediscovered at a new host or port after restart, Android restores that selection and updates hostUrl automatically
If multiple servers are discovered and there is no prior selection, Android does not auto-pick one or show a blocking chooser
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Android: show discovery state and manual fallback in Config screen
userStories.description: As an Android user, I want the Config screen to explain whether my current LAN endpoint came from discovery, a restored selection, or a manual URL fallback.
acceptanceCriteria: Config screen lists discovered servers with enough detail to distinguish them, including selection state and auth requirement
Config screen shows whether the active connection source is auto_discovered, restored_selection, or manual_fallback
When multiple servers are present with no prior selection, the screen shows passive guidance instead of a blocking chooser
User can explicitly switch between a discovered server selection and manual URL entry
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Shared fallback: preserve manual URLs when discovery fails
userStories.description: As a user, I want discovery failures to stay non-fatal so a valid manual URL keeps working when mDNS is unavailable or the selected server is not rediscovered.
acceptanceCriteria: If startup discovery finds no matching selected server, the client keeps any previously saved manual URL instead of erasing it
Discovery absence or failure does not block send or receive when the manual URL is valid
Clients expose a no servers found or discovery unavailable state distinct from multi-server waiting-for-selection state
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Diagnostics and regression coverage for DHCP discovery restore
userStories.description: As a developer, I need logs and tests around LAN discovery restore so DHCP-related regressions can be diagnosed quickly and kept from returning.
acceptanceCriteria: Clients log discovery start, resolved server_id, unique-server auto-select, restored selection, multi-server deferred-to-config, and no-match fallback events
Automated coverage exists where practical for unique-server auto-select, persisted server_id restore after host change, multi-server no-auto-pick, and manual fallback preservation
Manual verification checklist covers a DHCP-style host change scenario on macOS, Windows, and Android
Typecheck passes
